rulings.Case Title: DEBRA LEMMONS et al VS. JACKIE ROBINSON GARDEN APARTMENTS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS, ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULINGS ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. IN THE ALTERNATIVE, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS REFLECTED IN FACTS EIGHT AND EIGHTEEN. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION ONE IS SUSTAINED. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE CANNOT BE USED TO ESTABLISH THE TRUTH OF THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENT. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TWO AND THREE ARE SUSTAINED AS LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO FOUR IS SUSTAINED AS IT HAS NOT BEEN PROPERLY AUTHENTICATED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: THE CADLE COMPANY VS. KIM MICHAEL GALE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 12 PLAINTIFF THE CADLE COMPANY MOTION (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING)For Reconsideration: In reviewing this file, the Court notes that the original Application for Entry of Judgment on Sister-State Judgment attached certified copies of the original judgment of August 5, 1987 and the original writ of execution isssued on June 12, 1997. Those records were certified as true and correct by the clerk of the Clerk of the District Courts of Dallas County, Texas. As a consequence, the Court's prior ruling was in error to the extent it determined that plaintiff had not presented admissible evidence of those two records and it is, therefore, appropriate to consider plaintiff's motion for reconsideration. Plaintiff has estabished the terms of the Judgment and the fact that a writ of execution was issued within 10 years of the judgment as required by Texas law. With the motion for reconsideration, plaintiff, in addition to attaching the original judgment and writ, has attached a certified copy of the writ of April 6, 2007. Plaintiff's request to take judicial notice of these court records is granted. Plaintiff also has properly authenticated the assignments of the judgment as a business record of plaintiff and defendant's objection to paragraph 6 of the Patrick Declaration is overruled. A sufficient foundation has been established. Since the Court has taken judicial notice of Exhibits A, B and C of the Patrick Declaration it need not rule on defendant's other objections to the Patrick Declaration. In connection with the original hearing on this motion, defendant contended that plaintiff received only a partial assignment of $60,000. The Court has considered that argument and the applicable records and finds that plaintiff received a complete assignment of the Judgment of August 5, 1987. For the reasons stated, the motion for reconsideration is granted and defendant's Motion of September 10, 2
